name: Harrow International School Shenzhen
overview:
Harrow International School Shenzhen opened in 2020 in the Qianhai Co-operation Zone of Nanshan District in Shenzhen, Guangdong Province. The school is part of the Harrow family of international schools, affiliated with Harrow School in London, and was designed to accommodate a capacity of over 900 students from Early Years through Year 13 (ages 2–18). Both day and boarding places are available, with boarding fees charged separately from tuition.

The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout, with the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) curriculum in pre-school years, followed by the National Curriculum through primary and lower secondary, and then IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in the Sixth Form. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 in the 2025–26 academic year are RMB 342,100, among the highest of any British school in mainland China. The campus covers approximately 40,000 square metres and is located in the Qianhai Free Trade Zone.

campus.address: Northwest Corner of Tinghai Road, No. 3 Qianwan Road, Nanshan District, Shenzhen, Guangdong 518054, China
campus.context: Purpose-built 40,000 sqm campus in the Qianhai Co-operation Zone of Nanshan District, a free trade and innovation area in western Shenzhen.

name: The British School of Beijing, Sanlitun
overview:
The British School of Beijing, Sanlitun is a co-educational day school for students from age 1 through Year 6 (ages 1–11), located at No. 5 Sanlitun Road in the Chaoyang District of Beijing. It is operated by Nord Anglia Education and opened in 2009. The school is a primary-only campus, accommodating approximately 700 students. Families wishing to continue through secondary education typically transfer to the Shunyi campus.

The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout, delivered in line with the English Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) from Nursery through Reception, and progressing through Key Stages 1 and 2. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 in the 2025–26 academic year are RMB 307,000 (an early-bird discount reduces this to RMB 297,790 if the full year fee is paid in advance). Fees are listed in RMB and payments in foreign currency are converted at the Bank of China middle rate at the time of payment.

campus.address: No. 5 Sanlitun Road, Chaoyang District, Beijing, China
campus.context: Located in the Sanlitun diplomatic and commercial district of Beijing's Chaoyang area, close to embassies and the Sanlitun commercial zone.

name: The British School of Beijing, Shunyi
overview:
The British School of Beijing, Shunyi is a co-educational day school operated by Nord Anglia Education, located in the Shunyi District of Beijing near the Capital International Airport. Opened in 2003, it is the larger and older of the two British School of Beijing campuses, accommodating approximately 1,500 students from Pre-Nursery through Year 13 (ages 2–18). The school serves a significant proportion of diplomatic and corporate expatriate families based in the Shunyi area.

The Shunyi campus follows the English National Curriculum throughout primary years, transitioning to IGCSE in secondary and offering either the IB Diploma Programme or A Levels in the Sixth Form. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 in the 2025–26 academic year are RMB 307,000, identical to those at the Sanlitun campus. Fees are published annually in advance and payable in RMB. An optional school bus service is available at additional cost depending on distance from school.

campus.address: No. 10 Anhua Street, Shunyi District, Beijing, China
campus.context: Located in the Shunyi District of northeastern Beijing, close to Capital International Airport, in an area with a high concentration of diplomatic and corporate expatriate families.

name: Britannica International School Shanghai
overview:
Britannica International School Shanghai is a co-educational British international day school established in 2013 by Orbital Education, and is located at No. 1988 Gubei Road in the Minhang District of Shanghai. The school serves students from Early Years through Year 13 (ages 2–18) and has approximately 410 students enrolled. It is one of a small number of schools in Shanghai that explicitly follows the English National Curriculum throughout all year groups.

Students in secondary years sit IGCSE and progress to A Levels, with no International Baccalaureate track offered. The school's location in the Gubei area of Minhang District makes it accessible to families living in the Former French Concession, Jing'an, Zhongshan Park and Gubei neighbourhoods. Annual tuition fees for the 2024–25 academic year were RMB 299,800 for Year 3; fees for 2025–26 are expected to be similar, subject to annual review. The school's relatively small size reflects the limited demand for a pure English National Curriculum offering in Shanghai's competitive international school market.

campus.address: No. 1988 Gubei Road, Minhang District, Shanghai, China
campus.context: Located in the Gubei residential and commercial area of Minhang District, western Shanghai, near the Former French Concession.

name: Brighton College (Singapore)
overview:
Brighton College (Singapore) is a co-educational day school offering the English National Curriculum, operated by Cognita under the Brighton College UK brand. The Singapore campus opened in August 2020 at the Early Learning Village in Serangoon.

The school currently serves approximately 504 students from Pre-Nursery through Year 10, with Year 11 opening in August 2026. IGCSE first examinations are expected in 2027, with Sixth Form to follow.

campus.address: 1 Chuan Lane, Singapore 554299
campus.context: Located in the Early Learning Village (ELV), a shared campus facility near Lorong Chuan MRT on the Circle Line. The campus includes shared sports fields, pools, a 300-seat theatre, and a 25m swimming pool. Brighton College occupies its own dedicated teaching spaces within the ELV complex.

name: Nexus International School
overview:
Nexus International School is a co-educational day school in central Singapore, owned by Malaysia's Taylor's Education Group. The school opened in 2011 and operates from a purpose-built 12-storey campus in Aljunied.

It serves approximately 1,500 students from over 60 nationalities, from Nursery through Year 13. The school follows the IB Primary Years Programme and Middle Years Programme before transitioning to IGCSEs and the IB Diploma in Sixth Form.

campus.address: 1 Aljunied Walk, Singapore 387293
campus.context: Purpose-built 12-storey campus in the Aljunied area of central Singapore. Facilities include an Olympic-size pool with Omega timing system, two air-conditioned sports halls, a FINA-certified Astroturf pitch, two black-box theatres, 12 sound-isolated music practice rooms, and a double-storey library.

name: Tanglin Trust School
overview:
Tanglin Trust School is a co-educational day school in Singapore, established in 1925. It is the oldest British school in Southeast Asia.

The school follows the English National Curriculum and is the only school in Singapore to offer both A Level and IB Diploma pathways in Sixth Form. Tanglin operates as a not-for-profit trust, with all tuition fees directed back into the school.

Approximately 2,800 students from over 50 nationalities are enrolled across Infant, Junior, and Senior schools on its Portsdown Road campus.

campus.address: 95 Portsdown Road, Singapore 139299
campus.context: The campus sits in the Portsdown Road area, a quiet residential neighbourhood in western Singapore near one-north and Holland Village. The area is popular with expatriate families.

waitlistNote: Waitlists are common in popular year groups, particularly in Infant and Junior School.

name: Dulwich College (Singapore)
overview:
Dulwich College (Singapore) is a co-educational day school in Bukit Batok, western Singapore. Part of the Dulwich College International network affiliated with Dulwich College London, the school opened in August 2014.

It serves approximately 2,600 students from over 50 nationalities across three sub-schools: DUCKS (Early Years), Junior School, and Senior School. The campus spans five hectares and includes a 742-seat theatre, three swimming pools, and extensive sports facilities.

campus.address: 71 Bukit Batok West Avenue 8, Singapore 658966
campus.context: Purpose-built five-hectare campus in western Singapore. Facilities include a 742-seat performing arts centre, pipe organ, three swimming pools, a multi-purpose pitch, two gymnasiums, and a dedicated IB Centre. The DUCKS Early Years section offers a bilingual English-Chinese programme.

name: British International School Hanoi
overview:
British International School Hanoi (BIS Hanoi) is a co-educational day school operated by Nord Anglia Education, located in the Vinhomes Riverside neighbourhood of Long Bien District, Hanoi. Founded in 2012 as an extension of the BIS group that began in Ho Chi Minh City, the school has grown to approximately 1,100 students from 31 nationalities, spanning Foundation through Year 13 (ages 2–18).

BIS Hanoi follows the English National Curriculum in primary years alongside the International Primary Curriculum (IPC), with secondary students sitting IGCSE and progressing to the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 in the 2026–27 academic year are VND 715,500,000. The school's fee schedule includes school uniforms, daily lunches, snacks for Early Years and Primary students, learning materials, day trips and mandatory international examinations for secondary students.

campus.address: Vinhomes Riverside, Long Bien District, Hanoi, Vietnam
campus.context: Located within the Vinhomes Riverside gated residential development in Long Bien District, on the eastern bank of the Red River.

name: British International School Ho Chi Minh City
overview:
British International School Ho Chi Minh City (BIS HCMC) is a co-educational day school founded in 1997, originally as a small pre-school called Tiny Tots in District 1. It has since grown into one of the largest international schools in Vietnam, with approximately 2,300 students from over 55 nationalities across three purpose-built campuses in the An Phu area of District 2. The school is operated by Nord Anglia Education.

BIS HCMC follows the English National Curriculum and International Primary Curriculum (IPC) in primary years, with secondary students preparing for IGCSE examinations in Years 10–11 and either A Levels or the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 in the 2025–26 academic year are VND 705,200,000. Tuition fees include school uniforms, daily lunches, snacks for Early Years and Primary students, learning materials, day trips and co-curricular activities delivered by school staff.

campus.address: 246 Nguyen Van Huong Street, District 2, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
campus.context: Three campuses in the An Phu area of District 2, across Nguyen Van Huong Street: Early Years/Infant, Junior (Primary) and Secondary campuses.

name: Kellett School
overview:
Kellett School is a co-educational, not-for-profit British international school with campuses in Pok Fu Lam and Kowloon Bay, Hong Kong. Founded in 1976 by a group of parents seeking British-style education for their children, the school now enrols approximately 1,550 students from over 40 nationalities, spanning Reception through Year 13 (ages 4–18). The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout, with students in Years 10–11 sitting GCSE and IGCSE examinations and progressing to GCE A Levels in the Sixth Form.

As a not-for-profit association, all tuition income is reinvested into the school. Preparatory fees for the 2025–26 academic year are HKD 208,800 per year, placing the school among the more affordable options within Hong Kong's premium British school sector. Both campuses offer specialist facilities for science, arts, sport and performing arts. Students are drawn primarily from British and other English-speaking expatriate families, with approximately 40% speaking English as an additional language.

campus.address: 2 Wah Lok Path, Pok Fu Lam (Primary); 7 Lam Hing Street, Kowloon Bay (Secondary), Hong Kong
campus.context: Two campuses: the original Pok Fu Lam campus for primary-age students, and the Kowloon Bay campus serving both primary and secondary students, opened in 2013.

name: Shrewsbury International School Bangkok
overview:
Shrewsbury International School Bangkok is a co-educational British international school affiliated with Shrewsbury School in England. It was founded in 2003 and operates from two campuses: the Riverside Campus on Charoen Krung Road in Bang Kho Laem District, which sits beside the Chao Phraya River and provides education from Year 7 through Year 13 including boarding, and the City Campus in Huai Khwang District, which opened in 2018 for primary students in Years 1–6. Total enrolment across both campuses is approximately 1,900 students aged 3–18, drawn from around 40 nationalities.

The school follows the English National Curriculum in primary and lower secondary years, with students progressing to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels. For the 2025–26 academic year, Pre-Prep (Years 3–4) tuition fees are THB 878,100 per year, payable in three terms at THB 292,700 per term. Tuition includes lunch, morning and afternoon snacks, all books and library use. Boarding is available for secondary-age students at the Riverside Campus.

campus.address: 1922 Charoen Krung Road, Bang Kho Laem, Bangkok 10120 (Riverside); 982 Rim Klong Samsen Road, Bang Kapi, Huai Khwang, Bangkok (City)
campus.context: Riverside Campus on the Chao Phraya River in central Bangkok for secondary students; City Campus in Huai Khwang for primary years, opened 2018.

name: Harrow International School Hong Kong
overview:
Harrow International School Hong Kong opened in September 2012 on a site in So Kwun Wat, Tuen Mun, in the New Territories. It was the first British boarding school established in Hong Kong, and the third school in the Harrow family of international schools. The school accommodates students from Nursery through Year 13 (ages 3–18) and had approximately 1,300 students on roll as of recent reporting, drawn from around 35 nationalities.

The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout primary and lower secondary, progressing to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in the Sixth Form. Both day and boarding places are available. Annual tuition fees for Years 1–5 (which includes Year 3) are HKD 201,314 for the 2025–26 academic year, with an additional annual Capital Levy of HKD 60,000 per student. The campus is purpose-built on the site of a former army barracks, with dedicated facilities for academic, sporting and boarding provision.

campus.address: 38 Tsing Ying Road, Tuen Mun, New Territories, Hong Kong
campus.context: Purpose-built campus on the site of a former army barracks in So Kwun Wat, Tuen Mun, in the western New Territories.

name: Wellington College International School Bangkok
overview:
Wellington College International School Bangkok is a co-educational day school that opened in September 2018 following the Wellington College family of schools, which traces its origins to the founding of Wellington College in England in 1859. The school is located at 18 Krungthep Kreetha Road in the Saphan Sung District of eastern Bangkok and accommodates students from Pre-Nursery through Year 13 (ages 2–18). Enrolment is approximately 1,100 students.

The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout, with students progressing to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in the Sixth Form. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 are THB 855,000 for the 2025–26 academic year. The campus is purpose-built on a 10-acre site in the Krungthep Kreetha area, approximately 20 kilometres east of the city centre near the On Nut district.

campus.address: 18 Krungthep Kreetha Road, Saphan Sung District, Bangkok 10250, Thailand
campus.context: Purpose-built 10-acre campus in the Krungthep Kreetha area of eastern Bangkok, approximately 20 km from the city centre.

name: Nord Anglia International School Hong Kong
overview:
Nord Anglia International School Hong Kong (NAIS HK) is operated by Nord Anglia Education and opened in 2014. The school runs across multiple campuses in Lam Tin, Kwun Tong and Sai Kung, with the main campus located at 11 On Tin Street, Lam Tin. It caters to students from Nursery through Year 13 (ages 3–18) and has approximately 1,500 students enrolled across its sites. NAIS HK follows the English National Curriculum in primary years, with students progressing to IGCSE and either A Level or the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme.

For the 2025–26 academic year, tuition fees for Years 1–6 (which includes Year 3) are HKD 197,000 annually. New students are additionally required to pay a non-refundable, non-transferable Capital Enrolment Fee of HKD 100,000 on acceptance. The school is part of Nord Anglia's global network, which provides students with access to collaborative programmes with universities including MIT and Juilliard.

campus.address: 11 On Tin Street, Lam Tin, Kowloon, Hong Kong
campus.context: Main campus in Lam Tin with additional campuses in Kwun Tong and Sai Kung.

name: Brighton College Bangkok
overview:
Brighton College Bangkok is a co-educational British international day school that opened in 2016, affiliated with Brighton College in East Sussex, England. The school is located in the Krungthep Kreetha area of Bangkok and caters to students from Pre-Nursery through Year 13 (ages 2–18). Current enrolment is approximately 700 students. In 2024, the school announced plans to open a second campus, Brighton College Bangkok Vibhavadi, at 998 Vibhavadi Rangsit Road in Chatuchak District.

The school follows the English National Curriculum, with students progressing to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in the Sixth Form. Tuition fees for the 2025–26 academic year are THB 854,500 for Year 3. The fee schedule is published annually in advance as a formal PDF, and includes all tuition. Meals, uniform, examination fees and other extras are charged separately.

campus.address: Soi Pracharuamjai, Krungthep Kreetha, Bangkok, Thailand
campus.context: Purpose-built campus in the Krungthep Kreetha area of eastern Bangkok, near Suvarnabhumi Airport.

name: Harrow International School Bangkok
overview:
Harrow International School Bangkok is a co-educational British international school offering both day and boarding places. It is part of the Harrow family of international schools, affiliated with Harrow School in London. Located in the Lad Yao area of Bangkok's Chatuchak District, the school accommodates students from Pre-Nursery through Year 13 (ages 2–18). Founded in 2009, Harrow Bangkok is one of the earlier premium British-curriculum boarding schools established in Southeast Asia.

The school follows the English National Curriculum throughout primary and lower secondary, with students taking IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in Years 12–13. Annual tuition for Years 1–5 (including Year 3) is THB 852,600 for the 2025–26 academic year. Boarding is available for students in Year 6 and above, with full and weekly boarding options. The school reports some of the highest average fees among Bangkok's British schools, reflecting its dual day-and-boarding structure and extensive facilities.

campus.address: 45 Soi Kosumruamjai, Prachachuen Road, Lad Yao, Chatuchak, Bangkok 10900, Thailand
campus.context: Purpose-built campus in Chatuchak District, northern Bangkok, with separate day school and boarding facilities.

name: British School Jakarta
overview:
British School Jakarta (BSJ) was established in 1973 under the auspices of the British Embassy and has developed into one of Southeast Asia's leading international schools, with approximately 1,400 students from over 44 nationalities. The school is located in the Bintaro Sektor 9 area of Tangerang Selatan, Banten Province, with an additional Early Years centre formerly operated in Pondok Indah. BSJ is a member of the Federation of British International Schools in Asia (SEASAC).

Primary students follow the English National Curriculum for core subjects, with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC) used in Key Stages 1 and 2. Secondary students follow the IB Middle Years Programme (MYP) in Years 7–9, transitioning to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. Annual tuition fees for Key Stage 2 (Years 3–6) in the 2026–27 academic year are IDR 387,504,000, with a separate annual Capital Levy Contribution (CLC) of IDR 62,000,000. Fees cover tuition, textbooks and stationery.

campus.address: Jalan Raya Jombang-Ciledug, Bintaro Sektor 9, Parigi, Pondok Aren, Tangerang Selatan, Banten 15227, Indonesia
campus.context: Main campus in the Bintaro Sektor 9 development, a planned residential and commercial township approximately 20 km south of central Jakarta.

name: Alice Smith School
overview:
The Alice Smith School is a co-educational British international school in Kuala Lumpur, established in 1946 and named after its founder, Alice Smith, who started the school to provide British-style education for expatriate families during the post-war period. It is one of the oldest British schools in Southeast Asia. The school operates from two campuses: the Primary campus in Taman U-Thant and the Senior campus in Jalan Bellamy, both in central Kuala Lumpur.

Alice Smith follows the English National Curriculum throughout, with students progressing to IGCSE in Years 10–11 and A Levels in the Sixth Form. Annual tuition fees for Years 3–6 are RM 93,480 for the 2025–26 academic year, billed termly at RM 31,160 per term. Fees are subject to a 6% Service Tax for students whose annual fees exceed RM 60,000. Fees cover all books and materials; meals, transport and uniform are charged separately. The school is governed by the Alice Smith Schools Association, a registered not-for-profit body.

campus.address: 2 Jalan Bellamy, Bukit Petaling, 50460 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
campus.context: Senior campus at Jalan Bellamy; Primary campus at Taman U-Thant, both in central Kuala Lumpur.

name: Garden International School
overview:
Garden International School (GIS) is one of Malaysia's oldest private international schools, established in 1951 initially in Kuala Lumpur's city centre before relocating over subsequent decades. The main campus is now located in Mont Kiara, with an Early Years Centre at Jalan 41/70A in Desa Sri Hartamas. The school is co-educational and accepts students from Nursery through Year 13 (ages 3–18), with enrolment of approximately 2,000 students across both sites.

GIS follows the English National Curriculum, with students in secondary years sitting IGCSE and progressing to A Levels. Annual tuition fees for Years 3–4 are RM 93,150 for the 2025–26 academic year, billed termly at RM 31,050 per term across three terms. Fees cover technology resources, books and some stationery for Nursery through Year 9; uniform, transport, meals, school trips and examination fees are charged separately. The school is a member of the Council of International Schools (CIS) and the Federation of British International Schools in Asia (FOBISIA).

campus.address: Jalan Bukit Kiara Off Jalan Kiara 3, Mont Kiara, 50480 Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
campus.context: Main campus in the Mont Kiara residential district of northern Kuala Lumpur; Early Years Centre at Desa Sri Hartamas.

name: British International School of Kuala Lumpur
overview:
The British International School of Kuala Lumpur (BSKL) is a co-educational day school operated by Nord Anglia Education. It opened in 2009 and is located at No. 1 Changkat Bukit Utama in Bandar Utama, Petaling Jaya, a western suburb of Kuala Lumpur. The school accommodates approximately 1,510 students from Pre-Nursery through Year 13 (ages 2–18) from a wide range of nationalities.

BSKL follows the English National Curriculum, with secondary students sitting IGCSE and progressing to A Levels. Annual tuition fees for Year 3 are RM 90,240 for the 2025–26 academic year, payable termly at RM 32,343 per term. Fees are subject to a 6% Service Tax for students whose annual fees exceed RM 60,000. As part of Nord Anglia's network, the school provides students with access to global collaboration programmes, including partnerships with MIT and Juilliard.

campus.address: No. 1 Changkat Bukit Utama, Bandar Utama, 47800 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
campus.context: Single campus in the Bandar Utama residential township, approximately 15 km west of central Kuala Lumpur.

name: Discovery Bay International School
overview:
Discovery Bay International School (DBIS) is a co-educational day school located in Discovery Bay on Lantau Island, Hong Kong. It opened in January 1983 when Hong Kong Resorts International identified a need to serve the growing residential community in Discovery Bay. The school operates across three campuses within the Discovery Bay estate: an Early Years campus, a combined Primary and Secondary campus, and a Sixth Form campus in the North Plaza. Student numbers are approximately 1,000.

DBIS follows the English National Curriculum, with students progressing to GCSE and A Level examinations. Annual tuition fees for Years 1–6 (including Year 3) are HKD 143,700 for the 2025–26 academic year, placing it at the lower end of the premium British school segment in Hong Kong. The school serves primarily the residential community of Discovery Bay, which is accessible from central Hong Kong by ferry, and draws students from a wide range of nationalities.

campus.address: Discovery Bay Road, Discovery Bay, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
campus.context: Three campuses within the Discovery Bay residential estate on Lantau Island, approximately 30 minutes from central Hong Kong by ferry.

name: The Independent School of Jakarta
overview:
The Independent School of Jakarta (ISJ) is a co-educational day school in Pondok Indah, South Jakarta, operated by The Schools Trust — a UK-registered charity running 16 schools across 11 countries. The school opened in September 2021 and follows the English National Curriculum exclusively, with students progressing to IGCSE and A Level.

ISJ is structured around three sub-schools: Pre-Prep (ages 2–6), Prep School (ages 7–13), and Senior School (ages 14–18). All teachers are UK-qualified, and the school maintains a 1:9 staff-to-pupil ratio with class sizes of around 15 in Pre-Prep. Students come from over 30 nationalities.

ISJ is accredited by British Schools Overseas, recognised by the UK Department for Education and the Indonesian Ministry of Education, and listed in The Good Schools Guide. A purpose-built Senior Campus is scheduled to open in Pondok Indah in 2028. Eileen Fisher — a former ISI inspector with 31 years' experience — joined as Head in 2024.

campus.address: Jl. R.A. Kartini No. Kav. 12, Pondok Indah, South Jakarta 12310
campus.context: Located in the affluent Pondok Indah district of South Jakarta. The campus features a 25-metre swimming pool, a full-size football pitch, a theatre, dedicated science and creative arts spaces, and residential programmes at Woolverstone Hall in Suffolk. A purpose-built Senior Campus is scheduled to open in Pondok Indah in 2028.

name: Dulwich College Shanghai Pudong
overview:
Dulwich College Shanghai Pudong opened in 2003 in the Jinqiao area of Pudong New District. It is part of the Dulwich College International network, affiliated with Dulwich College in London, and was the first international campus in the network.

The school enrols approximately 1,600 students and runs the English National Curriculum from Early Years through Year 11 (IGCSE), then transitions to the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. Mandarin Chinese is taught from Year 1 onwards as a core part of the curriculum.

Admission is restricted to foreign passport holders only, in line with mainland Chinese regulations governing international schools.

campus.address: 266 Lan'An Road, Jinqiao, Pudong New District, Shanghai 201206
campus.context: Located in the Jinqiao expat residential area of Pudong New District, eastern Shanghai. The campus serves the large foreign community based around Jinqiao, Lianyang, and Biyun. Mandarin is taught from Year 1.

name: Dulwich College Shanghai Puxi
overview:
Dulwich College Shanghai Puxi opened in 2016 as the second Dulwich campus in the city, serving the western (Puxi) side of Shanghai. It sits in the Maqiao area of Minhang District.

The school enrols approximately 1,400 students and follows the same Dulwich International model as its Pudong sister school: English National Curriculum through Year 11 with IGCSE, then the IB Diploma Programme in sixth form.

Admission is restricted to foreign passport holders only.

campus.address: 2000 Qian Pu Jing Road, Maqiao, Minhang District, Shanghai
campus.context: Located in the Maqiao area of Minhang District, on the western (Puxi) side of Shanghai. Serves families based in Hongqiao, Gubei, and the Puxi suburbs.

name: Harrow International School Shanghai
overview:
Harrow International School Shanghai opened in 2016 in the Waigaoqiao Free Trade Zone in northern Pudong. It is part of the Harrow family of international schools, affiliated with Harrow School in London.

The school enrols approximately 800 students and runs the English National Curriculum from Early Years through Year 11 (IGCSE), then offers A Levels in the Sixth Form. AP courses were added in 2025 alongside the A Level pathway.

The school's location in the Free Trade Zone places it within reach of expat residential areas in northern Pudong including Jiading and Yangpu.

campus.address: No. 588 Gaoxi Road, Pudong New District, Shanghai 200137
campus.context: Located in the Waigaoqiao Free Trade Zone in northern Pudong, near the international port. The campus serves expat families based across northern Shanghai.

name: Wellington College International Shanghai
overview:
Wellington College International Shanghai opened in 2014 on a riverside site in the Qiantan (New Bund) development of Pudong New District. It is part of the Wellington College family, affiliated with Wellington College in Berkshire, England.

The school enrols approximately 1,660 students from over 50 nationalities and runs the English National Curriculum (with International Primary Curriculum elements) through Year 9, IGCSE in Years 10–11, and the IB Diploma in Years 12–13.

Wellington Shanghai is described as the largest international school site in downtown Shanghai. Forbes ranked it second among China's international schools in 2025; Hurun ranked it third in Shanghai.

campus.address: No. 1500 Yao Long Road, Qiantan International Business Zone, Shanghai 200124
campus.context: Riverside site in Qiantan (New Bund), Pudong, the city's newest financial and commercial district. Houses 50+ nationalities. Admission is restricted to foreign passport holders.

name: Nord Anglia International School Shanghai, Pudong
overview:
Nord Anglia International School Shanghai, Pudong (formerly The British International School Shanghai, Pudong) opened in 2002 in the Kangqiao area of Pudong New District. It rebranded under the Nord Anglia name as part of the group's global rebranding.

The school enrols approximately 700 students and follows the English National Curriculum at primary, IGCSE in Years 10–11, and the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. As part of Nord Anglia's network, students have access to global collaborative programmes including partnerships with MIT and Juilliard.

Admission is restricted to foreign passport holders only.

campus.address: 2888 Junmin Road, Pudong New District, Shanghai 201315
campus.context: Located in the Kangqiao area of Pudong New District, southern Shanghai, in a quieter expat residential area distinct from the Jinqiao or Qiantan hubs.

name: The British International School Shanghai, Puxi
overview:
The British International School Shanghai, Puxi (BISS Puxi) opened in 2005 in the Huacao area of Minhang District, on the western (Puxi) side of Shanghai. It is part of the Nord Anglia Education network.

The school enrols approximately 1,000 students and follows the English National Curriculum at primary, IGCSE in Years 10–11, and the IB Diploma in Years 12–13. The 2024 IB Diploma average was 34.5 points.

A Dutch international school is co-located on the same campus, an unusual setup that reflects the school's international community. As part of Nord Anglia, students have access to global collaboration programmes with MIT and Juilliard.

campus.address: 111 Jinguang Road, Huacao Town, Minhang District, Shanghai 201107
campus.context: Located in Huacao, Minhang District, on the western (Puxi) side of Shanghai. Serves families based in Hongqiao, Gubei, and the western suburbs.

name: Yew Chung International School of Hong Kong
overview:
Yew Chung International School of Hong Kong (YCIS) was founded in 1932, making it one of the longest-established international schools in Hong Kong. The school operates across three campuses in Kowloon Tong and enrols approximately 2,200 students from age 1 through Year 13.

YCIS follows the English National Curriculum from Early Years through Year 11 (IGCSE), then offers the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. The school's distinctive feature is its bilingual co-teacher model — pairing Western and Chinese educators in the same classroom — which delivers genuine immersion in English and Mandarin from the earliest years.

Debenture fees apply on enrolment in addition to annual tuition.

campus.address: 3 To Fuk Road, Kowloon Tong, Kowloon, Hong Kong (primary office; nine campuses across Kowloon Tong and Tseung Kwan O)
campus.context: Three campuses across Kowloon Tong, the established Kowloon expat residential area. The school's bilingual co-teacher model is unusual in Hong Kong's premium British school market.

name: German Swiss International School
overview:
The German Swiss International School (GSIS) opened in 1969 and operates two parallel streams under one roof: an English International Stream and a German International Stream. The English Stream follows the English National Curriculum through to IGCSE and the IB Diploma Programme; the German Stream follows the eight-year Gymnasium curriculum, culminating in the German International Abitur.

The main campus sits at 11 Guildford Road on The Peak, with a separate Kindergarten campus in Pok Fu Lam. Total enrolment is approximately 1,300 students.

The English Stream is the relevant track for British curriculum families. Year 3 fees sit in the lower band of Hong Kong's premium school segment.

campus.address: 11 Guildford Road, The Peak, Hong Kong (main campus); Pok Fu Lam Campus (Kindergarten)
campus.context: Main campus on The Peak with a separate Kindergarten campus in Pok Fu Lam. Suits families based in Mid-Levels, The Peak, and the southern district. The school runs both English and German streams.

name: Wycombe Abbey School Hong Kong
overview:
Wycombe Abbey School Hong Kong opened in 2019 as a girls-only British curriculum school, affiliated with the historic Wycombe Abbey School in Buckinghamshire, England. Located in Aberdeen on the southern side of Hong Kong Island, the school enrols approximately 250 students.

The curriculum is the English National Curriculum integrated with Chinese language and cultural elements. Students progress through IGCSE and on to A Levels in the Sixth Form, with the school recently expanding to a new senior campus to deliver education through Year 13.

Wycombe Abbey is one of the few all-girls British curriculum options in Hong Kong.

campus.address: Prep School: 17 Tin Wan Street, Tin Wan, Aberdeen. Senior School: 83 Sa Po Road, Kowloon City, Hong Kong.
campus.context: Located in Aberdeen on the southern side of Hong Kong Island. Convenient for families based in Aberdeen, Pokfulam, and Repulse Bay. One of Hong Kong's few all-girls British curriculum schools.

name: Bangkok Patana School
overview:
Bangkok Patana School opened in 1957 and is the longest-established British international school in Thailand. The school enrols approximately 2,300 students from age 2 through Year 13 on its single campus on La Salle Road in the Bang Na District of eastern Bangkok.

Patana follows the English National Curriculum from Foundation Stage through Year 11, with students sitting IGCSE in Years 10–11 and progressing to the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. The school's scale and heritage make it the default British curriculum choice for many long-term Bangkok expat families.

In addition to annual tuition, families pay a one-off entrance fee on enrolment plus a refundable deposit.

campus.address: 643 La Salle Road (Sukhumvit 105), Bang Na, Bangkok 10260
campus.context: Single campus on La Salle Road (Sukhumvit 105) in Bang Na District, eastern Bangkok. Established in 1957, the school anchors the Bang Na expat community.

name: St Andrews International School Bangkok
overview:
St Andrews International School Bangkok was founded in 1997 and now enrols approximately 2,300 students across its primary and secondary campuses in central Bangkok. The school is part of the Cognita group.

St Andrews follows the English National Curriculum from Foundation Stage through Year 11 (IGCSE), then offers the IB Diploma Programme in Years 12–13. The Primary campus is on Sukhumvit 71 in Phra Khanong; the Secondary campus is at Sukhumvit 1020.

The school is among the more accessible British curriculum options in Bangkok, sitting at the lower end of the premium fee tier.

campus.address: Primary: Sukhumvit 71, Phra Khanong, Bangkok. Secondary: Sukhumvit 1020, Bangkok.
campus.context: Two campuses in central Bangkok along Sukhumvit Road. Convenient for families based in Phra Khanong, On Nut, and the broader Sukhumvit corridor.

name: Denla British School
overview:
Denla British School (DBS) is a British curriculum school in Nonthaburi, on the western edge of Bangkok. The school traces its founding back several decades but now operates as Denla British under its current ownership, enrolling approximately 900 students from age 2 through Year 13.

DBS follows an enhanced English National Curriculum with specialist subject teaching from primary onwards, IGCSE in Years 10–11, and A Levels in Sixth Form. Thai curriculum components run alongside the British programme. Headmaster Jonny Liddell is a Cambridge graduate with NPQH qualifications.

DBS is accredited by the Council of International Schools (CIS), the Council of British International Schools (COBIS), and Cambridge International. ONESQA-accredited locally.

campus.address: 58 Moo 2 Ratchapruek Road, Om Kret, Pak Kret, Nonthaburi 11120, Thailand
campus.context: Located in Nonthaburi Province on the western edge of Greater Bangkok. Suits families based in the western and northern suburbs; less convenient for central Sukhumvit.
